Motion For Terminating Sanctions For Plaintiff?S Failure To Comply With Court Order Compelling Discovery
Off Calendar Aug-23-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

RE: DEFENDANT MING LAU's Motion For Terminating Sanctions For Plaintiff?S Failure To Comply With Court Order Compelling Discovery.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person.

The court adopts its tentative ruling. Off calendar. Defendant Ming Yuen Lau has filed a notice to withdraw motion for terminating sanction on 8/17/23.

Judge Pro Tem: Bruce Highman. Clerk: R. Woods. Not Reported. (302/JPT)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY

DEFENDANT MING LAU'S MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFFS RESPONSES TO DEFENDANTS DISCOVERY REQUESTS; REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS (ADCAL PER AGREEMENT OF PARTIES, EMAIL DATED 1/19/23
Off Calendar Mar-13-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. EDGAR W. HAWKYARD, Esq. of Jeanette N. Little & Associates (Tel: 925-225-6838) for defendant Ming Yuen Lau;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT MING LAU'S MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFFS RESPONSES TO DEFENDANTS DISCOVERY REQUESTS; REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS.

Defendant stipulates to the matter being decided by the pro tem judge. The pro tem judge and counsel for defendant (both appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Unopposed Motion to Compel is granted. Sanctions in the amount of $393.16 are awarded. Responses to be filed by March 31, 2023.

Order signed.

Judge Pro Tem: Robert Kane; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)
